name: La Font del Nogueret and la Font de Cabres (PR-CV 164) — Circular from Vall d'Uixó
name_original: La Font del Nogueret y la Font de Cabres (PR-CV 164) — Circular desde Vall d'Uixó
region_intro: Hiking around La Vall D'Uixó offers diverse landscapes, situated between the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park and the Mediterranean Sea. The region features mountainous terrain with numerous springs, ravines, and cork oak forests. Its varied ecosystems include steep rocky areas, high cliffs, and riverine environments along the Belcaire River. Best hiking trails around La Vall D'Uixó The most popular hiking route is La Font del Nogueret and la Font de Cabres (PR-CV 164) — Circular from Vall d'Uixó, a 9.1 miles (14.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 36 minutes to complete. This moderate route traverses ancient agricultural structures and unique springs. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pico Rodeno y Pico Pipa (PR-CV 241) — Circular desde La Vall d'Uixo, a difficult 10.0 miles (16.1 km) path. This trail ascends to Pico Pipa, offering panoramic views of the surrounding area. Local hikers also love the Hermitage of Sant Antoni Abad – Fortín de Dalt de la Cova loop from la Vall d'Uixó, a 3.5 miles (5.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 39 minutes. Hiking around La Vall D'Uixó is defined by mountainous terrain, cork oak forests, and views towards the Mediterranean Sea. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to challenging ascents. The routes in La Vall D'Uixó are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 150 reviews. More than 800 hikers have used komoot to explore La Vall D'Uixó's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in La Vall d'Uixó?
A: La Vall d'Uixó offers a wide selection of hiking opportunities, with over 60 routes recorded on komoot. These trails cater to various skill levels, from easy walks to challenging ascents, ensuring there's something for every hiker.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking options in La Vall d'Uixó?
A: Yes, La Vall d'Uixó has several easy routes suitable for families and beginners. There are 7 easy trails available, providing accessible ways to enjoy the region's natural beauty. These often feature gentler terrain and shorter distances, perfect for a relaxed outing.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see while hiking in La Vall d'Uixó?
A: Hiking in La Vall d'Uixó offers a diverse range of natural features. You'll encounter mountainous terrain, lush cork oak forests, numerous springs, and refreshing ravines, especially within the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park. The region also boasts steep rocky areas, high cliffs, and riverine environments along the Belcaire River, providing varied and scenic landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in La Vall d'Uixó?
A: Yes, many of the trails in La Vall d'Uixó are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e877927065/la-font-del-nogueret-and-la-font-de-cabres-pr-cv-164-circular-from-vall-d-uixo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Font del Nogueret and la Font de Cabres (PR-CV 164) — Circular from Vall d'Uixó&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 9.1-mile loop traversing ancient agricultural structures and unique springs.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can visit near the hiking trails?
A: La Vall d'Uixó is rich in historical and natural attractions. You can explore the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2880412" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of La Vall d'Uixó&lt;/a&gt;, an Arab fortress offering panoramic views. Don't miss the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1562770" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñas Aragonesas&lt;/a&gt;, or the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1813435" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos Negros Greenway&lt;/a&gt;. The famous Coves de Sant Josep, home to Europe's longest navigable underground river, are also a must-see.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in La Vall d'Uixó?
A: The hiking trails in La Vall d'Uixó are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.3 stars from over 150 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the well-maintained paths, and the stunning views, particularly from elevated points like Pico Pipa.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Absolutely. For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, La Vall d'Uixó offers 18 difficult routes. A prime example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e878878289/pico-rodeno-y-pico-pipa-pr-cv-241-circular-desde-la-vall-d-uixo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Rodeno y Pico Pipa (PR-CV 241) — Circular desde La Vall d'Uixo&lt;/a&gt;, a demanding 10.0-mile path that ascends to Pico Pipa, rewarding hikers with breathtaking panoramic views.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in La Vall d'Uixó?
A: La Vall d'Uixó is suitable for hiking year-round due to its Mediterranean climate. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, ideal for longer treks. Even in summer, the consistent 20°C temperature inside the Coves de Sant Josep provides a cool escape, and many trails benefit from the shade of cork oak forests. Winter hiking is also possible, with milder temperatures compared to more northern regions.

Q: Can I combine hiking with other outdoor activities in the area?
A: Yes, La Vall d'Uixó is a hub for various outdoor activities. Beyond hiking, you can enjoy cycling routes, explore the Coves de Sant Josep through speleokayaking or caving, and engage in birdwatching, especially along the Belcaire River. The region also hosts sporting events like duathlons and mountain races.

Q: Are there any trails that offer historical insights?
A: Many trails in La Vall d'Uixó combine sporting activity with cultural immersion. Routes like PR-V-164 traverse Muslim towers and ancient agricultural structures. You can also find trails that pass by heritage sites such as Muslim castles and traditional dry stone architecture, recognized by UNESCO. The "XYZ Line" trail explores a defense line from the Spanish Civil War, featuring trenches and defensive structures.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: Due to its diverse ecosystems, La Vall d'Uixó is home to various bird species, particularly along the upper part of the Belcaire River with its steep rocky areas and forest masses. The Sierra de Espadán Natural Park's lush cork oak forests also provide habitats for a range of local fauna, though specific sightings depend on the season and trail.
tips: From its 570 meters above sea level you will have guaranteed impressive panoramic views of the coast, the valleys of the Plana Baixa and in the distance those of the province of Valencia. Reaching the top is a very picturesque route and without too much technical difficulty, only at the end it will become a little more challenging but you will have the help of chains and ropes on the rock to assist you.
